title: Malabar Grey Hornbill
focus: Habitat, characteristics, and conservation status of the Malabar Grey Hornbill.
rationale: This is a distinct endemic species with specific physical traits and conservation statuses that are highly testable in UPSC prelims.
sectionExcerpt: Malabar Grey Hornbill Context: Researchers from Kerala were awarded Future Conservationist Award... Habitat: Endemic to Western Ghats of India. Characteristics: Lacks the casque typical of Hornbill; Loud, laugh-like call. Conservation Status: WPA, 1972: Schedule I. IUCN Status: VU (Vulnerable).
evidenceBullets: Endemic to the Western Ghats of India.
Lacks the casque typical of other Hornbill species.

title: Greater Flamingo
focus: Breeding grounds, habitat preferences, and conservation initiatives for the Greater Flamingo.
rationale: The notification of a new sanctuary in Tamil Nadu and the unique breeding ground in Gujarat's Kachchh make this a high-yield geography and environment topic.
sectionExcerpt: Greater Flamingo Context: Tamil Nadu notified Greater Flamingo Sanctuary at Dhanushkodi to preserve a critical stopover point along Central Asian Flyway. Habitat: Breeds in shallow wetlands that are either saline or alkaline... Kachchh Desert Wildlife Sanctuary in Great Rann of Kachchh (GRK) in Gujarat, is a unique Protected Area, supporting South Asia’s only breeding ground of Greater Flamingos... Characteristics: Great dispersal capacity outside breeding season, highly philopatric... Conservation Status: CITES: Appendix II. IUCN Status: LC (Least Concern).
evidenceBullets: Tamil Nadu notified the Greater Flamingo Sanctuary at Dhanushkodi.
Breeds in shallow wetlands that are either saline or alkaline.
Kachchh Desert Wildlife Sanctuary in Gujarat supports South Asia's only breeding ground of Greater Flamingos.
Highly philopatric species (return to or remain near a particular site).

title: Dugong
focus: India's Dugong Conservation Reserve, habitat distribution, and unique biological characteristics.
rationale: The establishment of India's first Dugong Conservation Reserve and its unique status as a marine herbivorous mammal are prime targets for UPSC questions.
sectionExcerpt: Dugong Context: IUCN adopted a motion recognizing India's first Dugong Conservation Reserve in Palk Bay... established in 2022, by Tamil Nadu government... Conservation Status: WPA, 1972: Schedule I. CMS: Appendix II. IUCN Status: VU (Vulnerable). Covered under Species Recovery Programme. Habitat: 40 countries from East Africa to the Pacific... India: Palk Bay (highest), Gulf of Mannar, Gulf of Kutch, etc. Characteristics: Only marine herbivorous mammals that depend on seagrass. ("sea cows").
evidenceBullets: India's first Dugong Conservation Reserve was established in Palk Bay by the Tamil Nadu government.
IUCN Status is Vulnerable (VU) and protected under WPA, 1972: Schedule I.
In India, they are found in Palk Bay, Gulf of Mannar, and Gulf of Kutch.
They are the only marine herbivorous mammals that depend on seagrass (known as 'sea cows').

title: Olive Ridley Turtle
focus: Mass nesting behavior (arribadas), conservation status, and Operation Olivia.
rationale: Olive Ridley Turtles are a recurring UPSC theme due to their unique mass nesting phenomenon and specific conservation operations in Odisha.
sectionExcerpt: Olive Ridley Turtle (ORT) Context: Operation Olivia shielded over 8-lakh ORT on Odisha's Gahirmatha coast. Conservation Status: WPA, 1972: Schedule I. CITES: Appendix I. IUCN Status: VU (Vulnerable). Habitat: Warm, tropical waters of the Pacific, Atlantic, and Indian Oceans. Characteristics: Undergo unique mass nesting called arribadas. Smallest and most abundant of all sea turtles.
evidenceBullets: Operation Olivia protects Olive Ridley Turtles on Odisha's Gahirmatha coast.
IUCN Status is Vulnerable (VU) and protected under WPA, 1972: Schedule I.
They undergo a unique mass nesting phenomenon known as 'arribadas'.
They are the smallest and most abundant of all sea turtles.
